creator: brett@peloton.physics.montana.edu
text:
This is a new port of latex2html-98.1p1.  This is a really
	handy tool to convert any LaTeX document into HTML.  The 
	script can handle inline postscript images and every 
	style of LaTeX document I know of.  In addition, style files
	for LaTeX documents to make better use of HTML commands are
	included.  I submitted a port of 97.1 way back in August of 
	last year and it went into the port black hole.  :-P

	Included below is a shell script which contains a uuencoded
	tarball of the new port.  It's been portlinted and passed thru
	okay on my home machine.

Fix: #!/bin/csh -f
# Uuencoded gz-compressed .tar file created by csh script  uufiles
# For more info (11/95), see e.g. http://xxx.lanl.gov/faq/uufaq.html
# If you are on a unix machine this file will unpack itself: strip
# any mail header and call resulting file, e.g., latex2html.uu
# (uudecode ignores these header lines and starts at begin line below)
# Then say        csh latex2html.uu
# or explicitly execute the commands (generally more secure):
#    uudecode latex2html.uu ;   gunzip latex2html.tar.gz ;
#    tar -xvf latex2html.tar
# On some non-unix (e.g. VAX/VMS), first use editor to change filename
# in "begin" line below to latex2html.tar-gz , then execute
#    uudecode latex2html.uu
#    gzip -d latex2html.tar-gz
#    tar -xvf latex2html.tar
#
uudecode $0
chmod 644 latex2html.tar.gz
gunzip -c latex2html.tar.gz | tar -xvf -
rm $0 latex2html.tar.gz
exit
How-To-Repeat: 
	extract port below, csh latex2html.uu, make install, then 
	commit!   :-)
